assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, and its foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the foreign currency exposure
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a recognised asset as an eligible hedged item in a fair value hedge, and paragraph 6.3.6 specifically allows foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the foreign currency exposure
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedging as a separate hedge accounting route for risks on recognised assets or liabilities, so it is a genuine designation route to assess for exchange-rate-driven variability in the receivable’s settlement cash flows.
